status: COMPLETE — design pipeline terminal; handoff to /build PRD-092

handoff.scope: Linux exemplar (Gate E1) — reference implementation only; Windows cert = follow-on PRD #2 (Gate E2).
build_obligations: Terminology/Operator-Copy Gate (PRD-092 §7a): local glossary, operator-copy review, submitted!=printed invariant, PrinterFault deferral — NOT SRL (srl_required:false).
GATE-PLATFORM-1 contract OS-neutrality is the trap-avoider: no cups types/semantics above adapter boundary.
ADR-063 enforcement NON-blocking here (only D5 idempotency + D6 failure reporting exercised); full D1-D7 enforced in PRD #2.
DEP-3 legal/layout + GAP-C5 barcode = provisional on exemplar; authoritative in PRD #2.

gate_resolution_2026_06_18.gate_hw_1: RESOLVED — UB-U05 USB (04b8:0202), no ePOS service; ePOS-direct (Option B) hardware-foreclosed. lsusb + CUPS URI usb://EPSON/TM-T88V?serial=4D5138467113430000 captured on dev box.
gate_resolution_2026_06_18.dep_os_1: RESOLVED — production OS = Windows; Linux/CUPS = dev adapter only.
gate_resolution_2026_06_18.transport: Option A managed local agent -> OS print spooler -> printer
gate_resolution_2026_06_18.observability: submitted-only; device faults out of scope (failure_domain=device null on both adapters)
gate_resolution_2026_06_18.adr_062: AMENDED — Deferred-Blocked T-1/T-2/T-3 -> Resolved; stale Linux-as-production framing corrected.
gate_resolution_2026_06_18.adr_063: AUTHORED — docs/80-adrs/ADR-063-loyalty-print-agent-deployment-standard.md (deployment/trust boundary ONLY; transport not reopened, per user constraint).
gate_resolution_2026_06_18.adr_063_scope_constraint: In: localhost trust boundary, install/update ownership, auth, idempotency, failure reporting, exposure prevention. OUT: transport re-eval, ESC/POS, spooler reconsideration, printer feature expansion.

two_phase_platform_sequencing.decision: ADR-062 D8 — prove on Linux exemplar first, then certify Windows. Separates feature bugs from platform/driver/permission bugs.
two_phase_platform_sequencing.gate_E1_linux_exemplar: GATE-PLATFORM-1 (contract OS-neutrality, NO cups semantics above adapter boundary) + GATE-PLATFORM-2 (fake+cups parity) + GATE-HW-2 (real-device on linux rig) + GATE-DOM-1 + GATE-DUP-1 + GATE-UX-1 + GATE-SEC-1(min). ADR-063 NOT blocking; only D5/D6 exercised.
two_phase_platform_sequencing.gate_E2_windows_cert: WINDOWS_CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED: windows_agent_exists + windows_spooler_adapter_exists + real_printer_test_passes + audit_contract_identical + localhost_security_review_complete. = GATE-PLATFORM-3 + GATE-PLATFORM-4 + DEP-OS-3 + full ADR-063 enforcement.
two_phase_platform_sequencing.anti_drift: Linux MUST NOT become permanent production assumption; rollout BLOCKED until E2 passes.

artifacts.prd_2_windows_cert: deferred — follow-on PRD for Phase 2 Windows certification (Gate E2)

phase_1_decisions.coexistence: lib/print window.print() utility retained for non-loyalty docs; window.print() superseded only on named loyalty redemption surface

phase_2_decisions.result_vocabulary: four_state_fixed (requested|submitted|failed|unknown); submitted = transport accepted; no completion claim
phase_2_decisions.print_attempt_model: single_audit_controlled_lifecycle_row (immutable identity/correlation, controlled status/fault transition); NOT append-only / NOT event log
phase_2_decisions.rollback: disables controlled printing on named surface; browser fallback requires explicit operational decision, never automatic

receipt_templating.adr_reconciliation: render/validation faults invalid_document|unsupported_operation vs canonical PrinterFault; four-state result fixed

context7_findings: no official Epson web SDK; community libs /klemen1337/node-thermal-printer (ESC/POS local-agent prior art) + /tr3v3r/react-native-esc-pos-printer; official ePOSBuilder API grounded from native manual

resume_context.where_we_are: Phases 0-4 COMPLETE. SEC_NOTE authored; FEATURE_BOUNDARY now carries an explicit ADR-058 Feature Classification block (CLS-007 external_integration + CLS-003 authoring, ADM-1..10). ADR-062 FROZEN (Accepted) after DA review: D1-D7 frozen, transport(#7)/OS(#8)/deployment-standard-ADR Deferred-Blocked. DA P0s + target/scope ambiguities patched (D7 discriminated canonical failure {failure_domain, failure_code}; D5 stable config key; D2 comp-slip claim narrowed; status note D1-D7). Phase 5 PRD is BLOCKED @GATE-HW-1.
resume_context.immediate_next_action: Phase 5 PRD is BLOCKED until GATE-HW-1 (inventory PRODUCTION printer interface) + DEP-OS-1 (production OS) clear. Two paths offered to user: (a) author PRD shell now with mandatory ADR-058 classification section + explicit GATE-HW-1 block; (b) PAUSE pipeline until hardware inventory done (recommended). PRD must include required_sections.prd from the classification standard verbatim.
resume_context.da_all_findings_resolved: All 6 DA findings applied. P0 x2 (status note D1-D7, D7 discriminated failure vocab) + P1 #4 (D5 stable config key) + P1 #5 (D2 comp-slip claim narrowed) + P1 #3 (D3 one production adapter per deployment, deployment-time not runtime hot-swap) + P2 #6 (D2 closed state machine: requested -> once to terminal submitted|failed|unknown; terminal rows immutable; new observation = new instance). ADR-062 fully frozen, Accepted.
phase_3_sec_note_focus: localhost print-agent trust boundary (GATE-SEC-1): localhost/LAN auth, no public listener, no secret in browser bundle
property-scoped printer_target; no device address exposed to browser
preview(HTML) vs production(adapter) payload integrity; receipt_document_hash provenance
RLS context via ADR-024 (operator_id, casino_id); print_attempt critical audit (session-var writes)
resume_context.remaining_pipeline: Phase 3 SEC Note -> Phase 4 ADR(s) (EXTEND ADR-045, not replace) -> Phase 5 PRD (BLOCKED until GATE-HW-1) -> handoff /build
hard_invariants_do_not_relitigate: Owner = LoyaltyService.InstrumentPrinting submodule (NOT standalone service; deferred)
Result vocabulary = four states requested|submitted|failed|unknown; submitted=transport accepted; NO acknowledged/completion claim
print_attempt = ONE audit-controlled lifecycle row per attempt (immutable identity/correlation, mutable status/fault); NOT append-only, NOT event log
coexistence: lib/print window.print() retained for non-loyalty docs; superseded ONLY on named loyalty redemption surface (GATE-UX-1)
templating = bounded supporting concern; FulfillmentPayload source input; ReceiptDocument canonical; HTML preview-only; no generic/runtime engine; not split until cross-domain/runtime-authoring
receipt_document_hash = digest of canonical serialized ReceiptDocument, NOT transport payload

blocked_decisions.transport: BLOCKED @GATE-HW-1 (inventory PRODUCTION printer interface) - provisional managed_local_print_agent; ePOS-direct(B) hardware-blocked on installed UB-U05 USB
blocked_decisions.production_os: BLOCKED @DEP-OS-1 (Windows assumed by addendum, but only Linux/CUPS adapter proven working today)

adr_reconciliation_items_for_phase4: render/validation faults invalid_document|unsupported_operation (templating guide §12) vs canonical addendum PrinterFault set - decide if they join
new ADR EXTENDS ADR-045-pilot-reward-instrument-fulfillment (supersedes only on loyalty redemption surface)
if local-agent transport chosen -> mandatory deployment-standard ADR (FIB-S adr_candidate)
resume_context.key_hardware_facts: Dev unit TM-T88V (M244A) on UB-U05 USB (04b8:0202) via one-way CUPS raster (rastertotmt88v) -> submitted-only, no device faults. ePOS-Print needs UB-E04/TM-i interface (not installed). ePOS returns ASB status AFTER print (could observe completion+faults) but pilot caps at submitted. Bidirectional ESC/POS (DLE EOT) needed for faults on agent path.
resume_context.current_print_surface_to_replace: lib/print/ (iframe-print.ts, print-reward.ts, templates/{comp-slip,coupon}.ts, hooks/use-print-reward.ts); FulfillmentPayload frozen at services/loyalty/dtos.ts:623; ADR-045. MTL modal components/mtl/mtl-entry-view-modal.tsx:445 OUT OF SCOPE.
resume_context.context7_status: back online but has NO official Epson web SDK (only community ESC/POS libs); native Epson PDFs are authoritative source
